cease to blow towards the promised land while they were upon the waters" (Ether 6:4-5, 8). Hugh Nibley has chronicled many "ancient" accounts of tremendous winds during the fall of Babel (Lehi 177-78). In the ancient world, wind was often seen as Jehovah's "instrument in overcoming chaos (Gen. 1:2; 8:1), and in transporting a prophet (1 Kings 18:12; 2 Kings 2:16; cf. 2:11; Ezek. 8:3; 11:1)"…

the point that judgment must come, destruction results; but it comes because of the removal of God's Spirit, prophets, and restraining hand. Thus it is by the wicked, unrestrained in their wickedness, that the wicked are destroyed (Mormon 4:5). The Book of Mormon testifies clearly of this point. Great are the promises concerning this land of America. We are told unequivocally that it “is a choice land, and whatsoever nation shall possess it shall be free from bondage, and from captivity, and from all other nations under heaven, if they will but serve the God of the land, who is Jesus Christ” (Ether 2:12). This is the crux of the entire matter—obedience to the commandments of God. (Gordon…

This verse can have several experiences lumped into one verse. 1) four years pass 2) Lord comes to the brother of Jared 3) the Lord stood in a cloud 4) talked with him 5) talked with him for three hours 6) chastened him for not calling upon Him. This verse does not necessarily say that the borther of Jared hadn't prayed for four years.

33 And Noah did ‘according to all that the Lord commanded him. And Noah was six hundred ‘years old when the flood of waters was upon the earth.
Noah did according to all that the Lord had commanded him. Although Noah had not yet seen any actual flooding, he still entered the ark in obedience with God’s command, as he believed His word.
